# ADR-0001: Unified recursive node with no first-round special case

> **Status:** accepted | **Date:** 2026-06-14

**References:** [ADR-0003](../adr/ADR-0003.md)

## Context

The optimization pipeline (optimization.py) iteratively improves an NPU model by
benchmarking, profiling, generating strategies, applying them, and recursing on
each successful fork. An early design risk was treating the baseline (the original,
unoptimized model) as a special first round — with its own bespoke setup distinct
from the recursive children. That split tends to grow divergent code paths: the
baseline gets profiled/benchmarked one way, children another, and bugs hide in the
seam between them.

The question: should the baseline be a distinct entry path, or just depth=0 of the
same recursion every other node runs?

This ADR records the chosen state; the rationale is reconstructed from the code and
its inline commentary (optimization.py:24-160).

## Decision

The baseline is depth=0 of a single homogeneous recursion. There is no first-round
special case.

Each node — baseline and every fork alike — runs the same sequence: (1) benchmark on
the real dataset to establish its own latency ruler; (2) profile→analyze→strategy to
diagnose hotspots and produce candidate optimizations; (3) fork child modes and
recurse. The winning child becomes the next round's base_mode, so optimizations are
ADDITIVE — stacked on the prior winner — not recomputed from the original model each
round.

Rationale: removing the baseline/child seam eliminates the class of bugs that lives
in the inconsistency between two code paths, and the additive structure is what lets
speedups compound across depth.

## Consequences

### Positive
- One code path for all nodes; no baseline-vs-child divergence.
- Optimizations compound: each depth stacks on the prior winner.
- Stop conditions (reached_2x, max_depth, no_strategies) apply uniformly.

### Negative
- The baseline pays the full benchmark cost even though it only sets the ruler.
- Recursion depth is bounded by MAX_DEPTH=5; deeper optimization chains are cut off.

### Neutral
- baseline_mode is threaded through the recursion as the correctness golden source
  (see [ADR-0003](../adr/ADR-0003.md)); the homogeneity here and the golden-anchoring there are separate
  decisions that happen to share the same recursion.

## Alternatives Considered

### Chosen: baseline is depth=0 of one homogeneous recursion. Every node (baseline included) benchmarks first to set its latency ruler, then profile→analyze→strategy to diagnose, then forks children and recurses. Optimizations stack on the winning child rather than restarting from the original model each round. (accepted)

- **Pros:** Single code path: no baseline-vs-child seam where bugs hide, Optimizations are additive (stacked on the prior winner), matching the goal of compounding speedups, MAX_DEPTH and stop conditions apply uniformly

### Rejected: special-case the baseline as a distinct first round with its own setup, then a separate recursive loop for children. (rejected)

- **Cons:** Two divergent code paths to keep in sync, Easy to measure baseline and children under subtly different conditions
- **Rejected because:** The seam between baseline setup and the recursive loop is exactly where measurement-inconsistency and selection bugs hide; homogeneity removes the seam entirely.
